2014 Finals Game 5: Spurs avenge 2013 Finals loss"},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_NBA_Finals","title":"2026 NBA Finals - Wikipedia","content":"The 2026 NBA Finals was the championship series of the National Basketball Association's (NBA) 2025–26 season and conclusion to the season's playoffs.
Under the first season with head coach Brown, the New York Knicks finished with a 53–29 record, their highest win total since 2012–13.
As the two-seed in the Western Conference playoffs, they defeated the Portland Trail Blazers in five games in the first round and the Minnesota Timberwolves in the Western Conference semifinals in six games.
Barnes won a championship with the Golden State Warriors in 2015 and made another appearance in 2016, Kornet won a championship with the Boston Celtics in 2024, and Olynyk reached the 2020 Finals with the Miami Heat.
Game 5 — June 13 — New York Knicks — 94–90 (4–1) — San Antonio Spurs
The Knicks overcame a 14-point deficit to win 105–95, marking the first time the Spurs ever faced a series deficit in the Finals.
They traded baskets on their next possessions until an Alvarado three brought the Knicks back to four points, 104–100, with 3:07 left.
On the back of a 45-point performance from guard Jalen Brunson, the Knicks ended their 53-year title drought with a 94–90 triumph in game 5.
They trailed by as much as 14 in game 1, 12 in game 2, 29 in game 4, and 16 in game 5.
Jalen Brunson became the eighth player to win the NBA Finals MVP after winning an NCAA Division I men's basketball tournament and fourth of those to have also won state high school championship, joining Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, Bill Walton, and Magic Johnson.
The official website of the City of New York."},{"url":"https://www.nba.com/watch/video/2026-nba-finals-history-is-calling","title":"2026 NBA Finals | History Is Calling | NBA.com","content":"NBA.comhttps://www.nba.com · Jun 3, 2026NBA.comhttps://www.nba.com · Jun 3, 2026The Knicks defeated the Spurs, 94-90, as they win the series, 4-1, and claim their first NBA Championship in 53 years.

The **2026 NBA Finals** was the championship series of the National Basketball Association's (NBA) 2025–26 season and conclusion to the season's playoffs. It was the Knicks' third NBA championship, and their first since 1973. The series ran from June 3 to June 13, 2026. The series was a rematch of the 1999 NBA Finals, which the Spurs won in five games, and of the 2025 NBA Cup championship game, which the Knicks won 124–113. New York won the first two games, a first in the Finals for the team. In Game 4, the Knicks rallied from 29 points down in the third quarter, and OG Anunoby won the game with a tip-in \"Tip-in (basketball)\") with 1.2 seconds remaining. The New York Knicks just kept coming back, erasing double-digit deficits in all five games and winning four of the five to earn the franchise’s first championship in 53 years. Jalen Brunson scores a Knicks Finals record 45 points in the closeout victory over the Spurs. Jalen Brunson led the Finals with 22 points scored in the clutch (with the score within five in the last five minutes of the fourth quarter), the most clutch points for any player in the Finals in the last 15 years (since Dirk Nowitzki scored 26 in the 2011 Finals).","score":0.64344186,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/48940704/2026-nba-finals-biggest-takeaways-highlights-san-antonio-spurs-new-york-knicks-championship","title":"2026 NBA Finals: Biggest takeaways from Knicks-Spurs Game 5 - ESPN","content":"# 2026 NBA Finals: Biggest takeaways from Knicks-Spurs Game 5. The 2026 NBA Finals have ended as the New York Knicks beat the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5 on Saturday. The Knicks stole home-court advantage in Game 1, defeating the Spurs 105-95 before taking a 2-0 series lead with a 105-104 win in Game 2. The Spurs bounced back in Game 3 with a 115-111 win at Madison Square Garden, while New York completed the greatest comeback in NBA playoff history, erasing a 29-point halftime deficit Wednesday to take Game 4 107-106. ## (3) New York Knicks eliminate (2) San Antonio Spurs, 4-1. **Biggest takeaway from the Spurs' performance:** Hard-nosed defense masked a stinker on offense, but not long enough to prevent San Antonio from blowing yet another double-digit lead in a 94-90 loss to the New York Knicks as the Spurs were eliminated in Game 5 of the NBA Finals.","score":0.63750535,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.7,"request_id":"b6057e41-c438-4374-a3d5-ab8c18bf1568"}
